source_intake_record.raw_question_text: In the feedback control system shown in <image> below, \( G(s) = \frac{6}{s(s+1)(s+2)} \). \( R(s) \), \( Y(s) \), and \( E(s) \) are the Laplace transforms of \( r(t) \), \( y(t) \), and \( e(t) \), respectively. If the input \( r(t) \) is a unit step function, then ____
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: \( \lim_{t \to \infty} e(t) \) does not exist, \( e(t) \) is oscillatory

choice_map.D: \( \lim_{t \to \infty} e(t) \) does not exist, \( e(t) \) is oscillatory

multi_step: solving requires combining the diagram structure with G(s)=6/[s(s+1)(s+2)] and a unit-step input

the result is in principle verifiable by standard control-theory calculations once the diagram is read
